summary: 3 FAIL, 4 WARN, 21 PASS. Paper does NOT compile clean despite user-reported status: 35 undefined-control-sequence errors in paper_draft.log (primarily \ket and \totaltests). All Lean theorem references resolve, all 8 bibliography entries are in CITATION_REGISTRY, and all verifiable structural/numerical claims match the FermiHubbardDimer.lean source.
blocking_issues: FAIL-1: \ket{...} command is used ~20 times but never defined (missing \usepackage{physics} or \usepackage{braket} or local \newcommand{\ket}). LaTeX log shows 33 of 35 'Undefined control sequence' errors from \ket — the paper will render with missing math content.

FAIL-2: \totaltests macro is referenced at paper lines 120 and 460 but is NOT defined in docs/counts.tex. The canonical counts pipeline (scripts/update_counts.py) emits \testfiles (file count), not \totaltests (individual pytest count). Paper body will render '~project tests' with nothing filled in.

FAIL-3: Paper claims '139 theorems in FermiHubbardDimer.lean' (abstract line 44, Section 4 narrative, Table 1 total, Section 6 line 554). Actual count is 141 top-level theorem+lemma declarations (all 'theorem' keyword, zero 'lemma'). The Section I breakdown 15+10+20+21+39+12+17+5=139 does not sum to 141 and does not match the per-wave distribution in the source (W2=32, W3=1, W4=8, W5=10, W5r2=12, W6A=20, W6B=7, W6C=11, W6r2=7, W7=10, W7r2=9, W6def=8, W8=6).

compilation_status.actual: compiles but with 35 'Undefined control sequence' errors and 1 undefined citation warning

numerical_claims.match: 1 - (2 / (Δ^2 + 4*t^2)) • Matrix.of (fun i j => darkVec t Δ i * darkVec t Δ j)

numerical_claims.location: Section 4, Fig caption / Table 1 W6C: U_SWAP eigenvalues {-1, +1, +1}, det=-1, trace=+1
numerical_claims.lean_theorem: U_SWAP_singlet_eigenvalues (1658), U_SWAP_singlet_det (1673) proves det=-1, U_SWAP_singlet_trace (1687) proves trace=1
E_plus / E_minus definitions; E_plus_value_at_zero (1857) gives E_plus(t,0) = 2|t|
E_plus_times_E_minus at line 1877: proves E_plus t U * E_minus t U = -(4 * t^2)
J_superexchange_bound at line 1980: hypothesis `4 * |t| ≤ U`, conclusion `|J_superexchange t U - 4 * t^2 / U| ≤ 16 * t^4 / U^3`

numerical_claims.location: Section 4: antisymmetric doublon eigenvalue = U via (0,1,0)

citation_integrity.notes: Registry notes DOI verified against Science 318, 1889

citation_integrity.compile_note: natbib issued 'Citation `Mathlib' on page 1 undefined on input line 88' warning due to revtex4-2 natbib mode; the \bibitem is defined later in the file but natbib resolves in order. Cosmetic issue but indicates the paper may not cross-reference properly without a rerun.

counts_macros.fix: Either (a) add \totaltests to update_counts.py and counts.tex (wiring pytest collection into the counts pipeline), or (b) replace \totaltests with \testfiles (file count, already emitted — value 58) and reword the sentence as 'tests across \testfiles test files' (semantics change).

structural_claims.location: Abstract line 44, Section 4 Table 1 total, Conclusion
structural_claims.notes: 141 top-level 'theorem'/'lemma' declarations. Discrepancy may reflect: (a) excluded wave-summary markers (13 marker theorems; 141-13=128, not 139 either), or (b) paper was drafted before 2 theorems were added. Re-count required.
Close but rounded low. '~2,500 lines' or '~2,540 lines' would be more accurate. Not blocking.

Paper's per-section numbers don't map cleanly to wave-boundary theorem counts. Paper may be grouping differently (e.g., counting defs or grouping across section boundaries). Table numbers should be regenerated by a count script rather than hand-coded.

structural_claims.actual: FermiHubbardDimer.lean grep sorry = 0; project axioms = 1 (gapped_interface_axiom in SPTClassification.lean)
structural_claims.notes: Paper correctly qualifies this at line 455 as 'a single axiom (gapped_interface_axiom in SPTClassification.lean, unrelated to this paper)'. 'zero new axioms' in the scope of this formalization is correct.

qualitative_claims.location: Abstract line 28, Introduction line 93, 'first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in any interactive proof assistant'
qualitative_claims.claim: first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in any interactive proof assistant
qualitative_claims.notes: Strong precedence claim. No systematic search documented. Before submission, verify against: Isabelle/HOL quantum libraries (isabelle-afp quantum), Coq QWIRE / QuantumLib, Lean Mathlib existing unitary/geometric-phase material. Paper qualifies scope ('algebraic core', 'discrete-step'), which is good defensive phrasing, but 'first in any proof assistant' should be backed by either a review or weakened to 'first in Lean 4' which is easier to defend.

qualitative_claims.location: Section 4 line 338, 'factor-of-2 difference underlies the observed 4× robustness'
qualitative_claims.claim: factor-of-2 sensitivity difference underlies the observed 4× robustness
qualitative_claims.notes: Hand-wavy connection: a factor of 2 in ∂-sensitivity does not straightforwardly imply 4× noise robustness. The 4× figure is from Kiefer2026 experimental data; the paper's 2-vs-1 derivative-ratio argument is a plausibility story, not a derivation. Consider softening to 'is consistent with' or 'contributes to' rather than 'underlies'.

qualitative_claims.claim: 'The first Lean formalization of the Fermi-Hubbard dimer singlet-sector spectrum' + 'The first formal proof, in any proof assistant, of the discrete-step statement underlying the Kiefer et al. SWAP gate'
qualitative_claims.notes: Same precedence caveat as above. Lean-specific first is defensible; any-proof-assistant first requires evidence.

scope_boundary_honesty.location: Abstract line 51, Introduction line 124, Section 5 line 408, Section 7
scope_boundary_honesty.notes: Explicitly and repeatedly stated that Kato's adiabatic theorem is NOT formalized; the paper proves the discrete-step statement conditional on the Kato reduction being valid.
Paper explicitly states Berry-connection holonomy picture is deferred to Target B / Layer 2.
Paper carefully frames the gate's geometric interpretation as resting on 'two independent finite-dimensional facts' (sign flip + dynamical phase vanishes), which matches the Lean bundle (W8e).

parameter_provenance.notes: Paper 18 does not invoke PARAMETER_PROVENANCE — it does not reference experimental parameters (no t, U, Δ numerical values from a BEC/optical-lattice setup). All physics parameters are symbolic. No provenance check applies. Status: N/A.

axiom_risk.notes: Project's single axiom is in SPTClassification.lean and is not in any dependency chain of FermiHubbardDimer.lean. Paper 18 correctly calls this out as 'unrelated to this paper' (line 456).

hypothesis_risk.notes: FermiHubbardDimer.lean does not rely on any Physics structure with hypothesis fields (FluidBackground, DissipativeCoeffs). All theorems are bare propositions over `t Δ U : ℝ` with optional `(t ≠ 0 ∨ Δ ≠ 0)` hypotheses. No HYPOTHESIS_REGISTRY entries apply. Status: N/A.

recommended_fixes_pre_figure_reviewer: FIX-1 (blocking): Add `\usepackage{physics}` OR define `\newcommand{\ket}[1]{|#1\rangle}` in the preamble. This is load-bearing for ~20 math expressions.

FIX-2 (blocking): Decide and implement totaltests semantics. Options: (a) add `\totaltests` to update_counts.py with pytest --collect-only parse; (b) swap to existing `\testfiles` and rephrase; (c) hard-code the number with a comment.

FIX-3 (blocking): Reconcile the '139 theorems' claim with the actual source. Either recount (likely 141) and update abstract+table+conclusion, or confirm the grouping convention the paper intends and add a footnote clarifying what is counted.
FIX-4 (important): Recompile after FIX-1/2 and verify zero 'Undefined control sequence' errors remain in paper_draft.log.
FIX-5 (optional): Soften 'first in any proof assistant' claims to 'first in Lean 4' unless a precedence search is documented.

verdict.reason: Paper does NOT compile cleanly — 35 Undefined control sequence errors from \ket (load-bearing) and \totaltests (canonical-macro drift). These must be fixed before visual review; currently the rendered PDF has missing math content. Note: Lean-side claims, citations, and structural claims are overwhelmingly sound — the paper is substantively correct, it just needs ~10 minutes of LaTeX cleanup plus a theorem recount.
